headline: Chip Surge Carries Thursday's Momentum Into Friday's Open
body_html:
Wall Street heads into Friday's open buoyed by a broad, semiconductor-driven rally that swept through Thursday's session. Advancing issues outpaced decliners by nearly three to one across our tracked universe — 292 names higher against 101 lower — with the semiconductors sector posting a gain of 4.19%, the sharpest advance of any group on the day. Arm Holdings surged 9.2%, Onto Innovation climbed 8.77%, Amkor Technology added 7.85%, and Allegro MicroSystems led the pack with an 11.49% jump, a collective move that traders will be watching to see whether it carries into the morning session.

The chip-led advance unfolded even as geopolitical uncertainty kept energy markets on edge. Oil is on track for a weekly gain as Middle East supply-risk premiums hold firm, while prediction-market participants see elevated U.S. gasoline prices persisting as long as Iran tensions remain unresolved. Energy was the lone sector in the red Thursday, off 1.01%, with Viper Energy and Chord Energy among the steeper single-session decliners.

Gold, meanwhile, is headed for a weekly loss as Gulf hostilities paradoxically reinforced rate-hike expectations, redirecting haven flows. Small-cap desks are also drawing attention, with action-oriented traders rotating toward that segment in search of the next momentum catalyst. Watch how semiconductor names open — any fade from Thursday's levels would be the first meaningful test of the rally's durability.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Complex Roars — But Can It Hold at the Open?
briefs.body: Thursday's session saw the semiconductors sector advance 4.19%, the strongest performance across all tracked groups. Allegro MicroSystems, Arm Holdings, Onto Innovation, and Amkor Technology all posted gains between 7.85% and 11.49%. Whether institutional buyers extend those positions at Friday's open or trim into strength is the morning's central question.

briefs.headline: IEA Warns U.S.-Iran Escalation Could Upend 2027 Oil Surplus
briefs.body: The International Energy Agency cautioned that a further deterioration in U.S.-Iran relations carries the potential to disrupt what was otherwise expected to be an oversupplied global oil market in 2027. The warning adds a longer-dated dimension to a risk narrative that has already lifted crude prices toward a weekly gain.

briefs.headline: Hormuz Traffic Continues Despite Heightened Regional Tensions
briefs.body: LNG carriers and Japan-affiliated vessels continued transiting the Strait of Hormuz even as Mideast hostilities flared anew, suggesting energy supply chains remain intact for now. Markets will monitor whether that passage stays open, given its outsized role in global liquefied natural gas flows and oil export volumes.

briefs.headline: Gold Set for Weekly Decline as Rate-Hike Bets Absorb Haven Demand
briefs.body: Rather than lifting gold, the latest Gulf tensions appear to have reinforced market expectations for additional rate increases, weighing on the precious metal heading into the weekly close. The dynamic illustrates an unusual risk environment in which geopolitical stress is being read as inflationary rather than deflationary.

briefs.headline: Anthropic Names Former Fed Chair Bernanke to Independent Trust
briefs.body: Artificial-intelligence startup Anthropic appointed former Federal Reserve Chairman Ben Bernanke to its independent trust, a governance move that signals the company's intent to bring high-profile institutional credibility to its oversight structure. The appointment is likely to draw fresh attention to AI governance debates across the technology sector.

briefs.headline: Small-Cap Desks Stir as Traders Hunt for the Next Momentum Trade
briefs.body: With mega-cap semiconductors having already posted outsized Thursday gains, some traders are shifting focus toward smaller-capitalization names in search of fresh momentum. The rotation, if it gains traction Friday, could broaden market participation beyond the chip complex that dominated Thursday's advance.
